user_query:
I need a comprehensive warehouse operations optimization plan. Please read the following files under input/ and build a data-driven action plan:
- input/warehouse_profile.yaml
- input/order_volume.csv
- input/sku_velocity.csv
- input/current_metrics.json

Deliverables to write under output/:
1) output/plan.json — A prioritized action plan with three tiers: quick wins (0–30 days, <$5K), medium-term (30–90 days, $5K–$50K), and strategic investments (90+ days, >$50K). Include at least 4 quick wins, 3 medium-term items, and 3 strategic items. Each item must include: title, expected_roi_percent, investment_cost_usd, timeline_days, and resources (array). Ensure costs and timelines align with the tier definitions.
2) output/analysis_summary.md — A narrative assessment with these exact section headers: Space Utilization Audit; Pick Path Optimization; Labor Productivity Metrics; Inventory Accuracy; Cost Per Order Analysis; Automation ROI Calculator; Safety & Compliance. Include numeric lines for "Cubic utilization:" and "Travel time % of pick time:". Reference at least one automation option by name (e.g., pick-to-light, conveyor, AS/RS, AMRs/AGVs, sortation) and mention DART rate and cycle counting.
3) output/cost_per_order.csv — A CSV with columns: category,basis,cost_usd. Include exactly these categories with benchmark-aligned costs: Receiving (per unit: $0.30–$0.80), Storage (per pallet-month: $8–$15), Pick & Pack (per order: $1.50–$4.00), Shipping (per order: positive value), Returns (per return: $5–$15). Use the specified basis strings in parentheses as the basis column values.
4) output/abc_summary.json — Summary of ABC analysis derived from sku velocity, with fields: A_percent, B_percent, C_percent (numbers), and A_items_near_packstations (boolean reflecting the recommendation that A items be within 50 ft of pack stations).
5) output/assumptions.json — Any assumptions you needed to make (array under key "assumptions", at least 3 items).
6) output/rubric_notes.md — A prose explanation of your rationale, calculations, and prioritization logic (at least 800 characters), including explicit risk and feasibility notes.

Prioritize by ROI and feasibility, tie recommendations to the provided data, and call out any risks or dependencies.
